page.title: Agents
page.tagline: AI teammates that pick up issues, comment, and update status.
page.list_load_failed_default: Something went wrong fetching the agent list.

empty.title: No agents yet
empty.description: Create an agent and assign it issues, like any teammate. Local agents run on your machine; cloud agents run on Multica's runtime.

no_matches.title: No matches
no_matches.search_active_filtered: No agents match "{{query}}" in this filter.

row_actions.cancel_dialog_no_tasks: There are no active tasks to cancel.
row_actions.cancel_dialog_running_note: Running tasks may take up to 5 seconds to fully halt.
row_actions.archive_dialog_description: The agent won't be assignable or mentionable, and any active tasks will be cancelled. All history is preserved and you can restore it later.

detail.not_found_default: This agent may have been archived or deleted.
detail.no_access_title: You don't have access to this agent
detail.no_access_hint: Only the agent owner or a workspace admin can view this private agent.
detail.archived_banner: This agent is archived. It cannot be assigned or mentioned.
detail.archive_dialog_description: "{{name}}" will be archived. It won't be assignable or mentionable, but all history is preserved. You can restore it later.

pickers.model_search_placeholder: Search or type a model ID
pickers.model_custom_tooltip: Use "{{value}}" as a custom model id
pickers.model_clear_title: Clear and fall back to the runtime's provider default
pickers.thinking_clear_title: Clear the override and let the local CLI config (claude/codex) decide the reasoning level

model_dropdown.label: Model
model_dropdown.managed_by_runtime_title: Model selection is managed by this runtime.
model_dropdown.managed_by_runtime_hint: Configure the model on the runtime host (e.g. Hermes reads it from its own config file).

tabs.discard_dialog_description: You have unsaved changes in this tab. Leaving now will discard them.

create_dialog.description_create: Create a new AI agent for your workspace.
create_dialog.description_duplicate: Create a new agent based on "{{name}}". Instructions, env, and skills are copied for you.
create_dialog.runtime_private_locked_tooltip: Private runtime — only its owner or a workspace admin can create agents on it. Ask the owner to switch it to Public to share.
create_dialog.skill_attach_failed_toast: Agent created, but failed to attach skills: {{error}}
create_dialog.squad_join_failed_toast: Agent "{{name}}" was created but couldn't join the squad: {{error}}. You can add it manually from Members.

instructions.label: Instructions
instructions.placeholder_duplicate: Click to view or edit the duplicated instructions
instructions.editor_placeholder: Write what this agent should do, what to focus on, what to avoid…

skills_section.label: Skills
skills_section.list_empty_multi: No skills in this workspace yet. Create or import one first.

instructions.intro: Define this agent's identity and working style. Injected into the agent's context for every task. Markdown is supported.
instructions.placeholder:
Define this agent's role, expertise, and working style.

# Example
You are a frontend engineer specializing in React and TypeScript.

## Working Style
- Write small, focused PRs — one commit per logical change
- Prefer composition over inheritance
- Always add unit tests for new components

## Constraints
- Do not modify shared/ types without explicit approval
- Follow the existing component patterns in features/

env.intro_readonly: Injected into the agent process at launch. Only workspace owners and admins can read or edit values; every reveal and edit is recorded in the workspace audit log.
env.intro_prefix: Injected into the agent process at launch (e.g.
env.not_revealed_hint: Values stay masked until you reveal them. Every reveal and edit is recorded in the workspace audit log.
env.empty_editable: No environment variables configured. Add one to inject it into the agent at launch.

custom_args.intro: Additional CLI arguments appended to the agent command at launch. Multi-token flags can share one row — they'll be split on whitespace before being passed to the CLI.

mcp_config.intro: MCP server configuration forwarded to the runtime CLI (Claude via --mcp-config, Codex via $CODEX_HOME/config.toml, Hermes/Kimi/Kiro via ACP mcpServers, OpenCode via OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT, OpenClaw via the per-task config wrapper). Stored verbatim and may contain secrets — only the agent owner and workspace admins can read it. Leave empty to fall back to the CLI's own default. For OpenClaw HTTP/SSE entries use OpenClaw's own field name (e.g. "transport": "streamable-http") instead of Claude's "type".
mcp_config.invalid_not_object: MCP config must be a JSON object (e.g. {"mcpServers": …}).
mcp_config.redacted_hint: Only the agent owner or a workspace admin can read this config.

skills.intro: Workspace skills assigned to this agent. Local runtime skills are always available automatically.
skills.empty_hint: Add workspace skills to share team knowledge with this agent.
skills.add_dialog_description: Select a workspace skill to assign to this agent.
skills.add_dialog_empty: All workspace skills are already assigned.
skills.add_dialog_empty_partial: No more skills to add — this agent already has all of them.

integrations.intro: Connect this agent to external chat platforms so people can work with it where they already are.
integrations.members_note: Only workspace owners and admins can bind a Lark Bot to an agent. You can view connected bots in Settings → Integrations.

activity.empty_now: This agent isn't running anything right now.
activity.empty_30d: No completions in the last 30 days.
activity.empty_recent: This agent hasn't completed anything yet.

private.label: Personal
private.tooltip: Personal · only you and workspace admins can use this agent

workspace.label: Workspace
workspace.tooltip: Workspace · everyone in this workspace can use this agent

bulk_edit.custom_arg_help: Removing this row removes it from the replacement list. Example: --model claude-sonnet-4 is saved as two args. The agents are not changed until you click Apply.
bulk_edit.custom_arg_add_help: Add this arg to each targeted agent if it is not already present. Multi-token input is split on whitespace; example: --max-turns 100 adds --max-turns and 100.
bulk_edit.custom_arg_replace_help: Replace one exact existing arg with one replacement arg on each targeted agent. Agents without the existing arg are left unchanged.
bulk_edit.custom_arg_remove_help: Remove this exact arg from each targeted agent where it exists. Agents without it are left unchanged.
bulk_edit.existing_args_help: Choose an arg to fill the next empty row as Replace by default, or type directly in any row. The operations below change targeted agents only when you click Apply.
bulk_edit.env_keys_empty: No existing env keys for these agents. Type a new key.
bulk_edit.env_keys_load_failed: Couldn't load existing env keys. You can still type a key manually.
bulk_edit.existing_env_keys_help: Choose a key to fill the next empty env row, or type a key directly. Then choose Set / replace or Remove in that row. Existing values are never loaded.
bulk_edit.env_set_help: Set API_KEY to a new value by selecting it from suggestions, or type NEW_KEY to create it on targeted agents. If the key already exists, its value is replaced; if it is missing, it is added.
bulk_edit.env_remove_help: Remove OLD_KEY from agents where it exists by selecting it from suggestions or typing it manually. Agents without that key are left unchanged. No env values are read before this operation.
bulk_edit.local_presets_help: Presets are saved only in this browser for the current workspace. They restore field choices, model, runtime, custom args, and env key operations. Env values are never saved; set / replace rows need fresh values after loading a preset.
bulk_edit.local_presets_description: Saved in this browser for this workspace. Env values are not saved.
bulk_edit.preset_env_values_required: This preset restored env keys only. Enter new values before applying set / replace rows.

help.dialog: Choose only the fields to change. Disabled fields are left unchanged. Example: enable Model and leave it empty to clear model overrides and follow the runtime provider default. Env suggestions contain key names only; existing values are never loaded here.
help.runtime: When enabled, selected agents move to this runtime. Leave disabled to keep each agent's current runtime. Example: enable Runtime only to move agents without changing their model.
help.model: When enabled, this writes the model override. An empty value clears the override so the agent follows the runtime provider default. Example: enter gpt-5.5 to pin that model.
help.max_concurrent_tasks: When enabled, this replaces the max concurrent task limit for every targeted agent. Example: 1 runs one task at a time; 3 allows three parallel tasks.
help.custom_args: When enabled, this applies Add, Replace, or Remove operations to each targeted agent's existing custom args. Add uses the same whitespace splitting as the Custom Args tab; existing arg suggestions, Replace, and Remove target exact existing arg values. Empty rows are ignored, and nothing changes until you click Apply.
help.env: Use Set / replace to add a missing key or replace an existing value. Use Remove to delete a key where it exists. The key field suggests existing keys but also accepts a new key. Existing values are not read. If the same key appears more than once, the last row wins.
